otndata 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Access for ACT Network members

Create summary reports of receiver project data from the OTN data push.

Source: R/receiver_summary.R
otn_receiver_summary.Rd

Create summary reports of receiver project data from the OTN data push.

Usage

otn_receiver_summary(
  project = NULL,
  server = NULL,
  qualified = NULL,
  unqualified = NULL,
  deployment = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

project

Character. The project code.

server

Character. URL of the OTN-style Plone server. Defaults to the main OTN server at https://members.oceantrack.org.

qualified, unqualified

Default is NULL: OTN qualified or unqualified detections in parquet format will be downloaded from the data server. This argument also accepts a character vector of file paths of your qualified/unqualified detections if you do not wish to download your files or your node does not use an OTN-style Plone CMS.

deployment

File path of user-supplied master OTN receiver deployment metadata.

...

Arguments passed to otndo::make_receiver_push_summary

No files provided

If you only provide your project code and leave all of the arguments as their defaults, this function will ask you to log in then proceed to download all of the necessary files. You can speed up this process substantially by providing already-downloaded files.

Output

This function creates an HTML report that can be viewed in your web browser.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Using only the project code:
otn_receiver_summary("tailwinds")

# Providing a local file:
otn_receiver_summary("tailwinds", deployment = "my_master_deployment_metadata.xlsx")

# Get a summary fo what has changed since a particular date:
otn_receiver_summary("tailwinds", since = "2022-05-01")
} # }
